seed_policy.rule: Randomized tests must use an explicit seed, record that seed, and generate a reproducible case fingerprint.

artifact_policy.rule: Every monkey, stress, benchmark, or repeated-variance run must produce a machine-readable artifact tied to the build set.

variance_policy.rule: Repeated runs must compare stable summaries and fail, warn, or record a blocker when status vectors, health states, or benchmark envelopes drift beyond the scenario tolerance.

scenarios.checks: Seeded Engine API fuzz sequence, deterministic case fingerprint, repeated status-vector comparison, and malformed input rejection.
Seeded UI event stream without rotation events, no ANR/crash dialog, daemon still reachable, and final Engine API health snapshot.
Repeated direct runtime exec, path rewriting, memory guard, argv preservation, and process cleanup under a prepared rootfs.
Runtime syscall overhead, process startup, filesystem-heavy workload timing, and stable artifact capture.

Planned Android-full survival replay: large allocation denial should return ENOMEM before LMK, record memory telemetry, keep daemon reachable, and preserve enough evidence for restart-time classification. Current fast evidence covers the direct memory-guard contract and telemetry schema separately.
Repeated llama GPU comparison, bridge overhead summary, blocker classification, and benchmark drift evidence.
Host/container GPU bridge comparison, repeatable benchmark artifact, and performance regression tracking.
